text: Which sentence uses 'affect' correctly as a verb?
options: The new policy had a positive affect on sales.
The weather can seriously affect crop production.
What will be the long-term affect of this decision?
She showed no emotional affect during the trial.
hint: C1: 'Affect' as verb = to influence. 'Effect' as noun = result.

text: Which sentence uses 'effect' correctly as a noun?
options: The new law will effect significant changes.
The medicine had an immediate effect.
How will this effect our plans?
They hope to effect a peaceful resolution.
hint: C1: 'Effect' as noun = result. 'Effect' as verb = to bring about.

text: Choose the correct word: 'He is ____ to the manager.'
hint: C1: 'Accountable to someone' = responsible to them.

text: Which sentence uses 'compare to' correctly (meaning similarity)?
options: Shakespeare compared life to a stage.
Compare this report with the previous one.
Compared to last year, sales are up.
hint: C1: 'Compare to' emphasizes similarity between different things.

text: Which sentence uses 'compare with' correctly (meaning differences)?
options: She compared him to a lion.
If you compare this with that, you'll see the difference.
Life is often compared to a journey.
He compared her to an angel.
hint: C1: 'Compare with' emphasizes differences between similar things.

text: Choose the correct form: 'She is ____ than her sister.'
hint: C1: One-syllable adjectives take -er in comparative.

text: Which sentence uses 'different from' correctly in standard English?
hint: C1: 'Different from' is standard; 'different than' is American informal.

text: Choose the correct word: 'Please ____ the document before signing.'
hint: C1: All are acceptable, with slight nuance differences.

text: Which sentence uses 'each' and 'every' correctly?
hint: C1: 'Each' and 'every' are singular; verb must be singular.

text: Choose the correct spelling:
hint: C1: 'Separate' – remember 'there is a rat in separate'.

text: Which sentence uses 'due to' correctly after a form of 'to be'?
options: The cancellation was due to the rain.
Due to the rain, the game was cancelled.
The game was cancelled due to the rain.
hint: C1: 'Due to' is now acceptable in all positions.

text: Choose the correct word: 'She ____ the opportunity to study abroad.'
hint: C1: All collocate with 'opportunity' with similar meaning.

text: Which sentence uses 'whether' correctly?
options: I don't know if whether he will come.
Whether or not it rains, we will go.
I wonder whether if he is ready.
Whether he will come or not is uncertain.
hint: C1: 'Whether or not' introduces a condition; avoid 'if whether'.

text: Which sentence uses 'if' correctly (not 'whether')?
options: I don't know if he is coming.
If it rains, we will stay home.
I wonder if he is ready.
hint: C1: 'If' for conditionals; both 'if' and 'whether' for indirect questions.

text: Choose the correct word: 'He is ____ in the hospital.'
hint: C1: 'In the hospital' (patient), 'at the hospital' (visitor or worker).

text: Which sentence uses 'historic' and 'historical' correctly?
options: This is a historic moment for our country.
The museum has many historical artifacts.
hint: C1: 'Historic' = important; 'historical' = relating to history.

text: Choose the correct word: 'She is ____ to succeed.'
hint: C1: 'Liable' (negative), 'likely' (neutral), 'apt' (characteristic).

text: Which sentence uses 'comprise' correctly?
options: The team comprises of ten players.
The team is comprised of ten players.
hint: C2: 'Comprise' = contain; 'is comprised of' is controversial but common.

text: Which sentence uses 'compose' correctly?
options: The team is composed of ten players.
The team composes of ten players.
hint: C1: 'Be composed of' or 'compose' (active).

text: Choose the correct word: 'He is ____ to criticism.'
hint: C1: 'Sensitive' = easily affected; 'sensible' = practical.

text: Which sentence uses 'continual' and 'continuous' correctly?
options: The continual rain lasted all day without stopping.
The continuous interruptions made work impossible.
The continuous noise from the factory never stops.
hint: C1: 'Continuous' = without interruption; 'continual' = frequently repeated.

text: Choose the correct word: 'She gave me some ____ advice.'
hint: C1: 'Invaluable' = extremely useful; 'priceless' = precious.

text: Which sentence uses 'fewer' and 'less' correctly?
options: There are less people here today.
I have fewer money than you.
There are fewer cars on the road.
I have less apples than you.
hint: C1: 'Fewer' for countable nouns; 'less' for uncountable.

text: Choose the correct word: 'He made a ____ mistake.'
hint: C1: All collocate with 'mistake' with similar meaning.

text: Which sentence uses 'principal' and 'principle' correctly?
options: The school principal spoke to us.
hint: C1: 'Principal' = main or head; 'principle' = rule or belief.

text: Choose the correct word: 'The contract is ____.'
hint: C1: Fixed legal collocation: 'null and void'.

text: Which sentence uses 'alternate' and 'alternative' correctly?
options: We have no alternative but to leave.
The days alternate between sun and rain.
hint: C1: 'Alternate' = every other or rotate; 'alternative' = choice.

text: Choose the correct word: 'She is ____ to colds.'
hint: C1: All mean 'likely to be affected by'.

text: Which sentence uses 'anticipate' correctly?
options: I anticipate that it will rain.
hint: C1: 'Anticipate' = expect or prepare for.

text: Choose the correct word: 'He is a ____ of the arts.'
hint: C1: All mean 'supporter' with different nuances.

text: Which sentence uses 'enormity' correctly?
options: The enormity of the task overwhelmed us.
The enormity of the crime shocked everyone.
The enormity of the building was impressive.
hint: C2: 'Enormity' = great evil or scale; for size, 'enormousness' is safer.

text: Choose the correct word: 'The ____ of the situation was clear.'
hint: C1: All mean 'importance/danger'.

text: Which sentence uses 'infer' and 'imply' correctly?
options: He implied that I was lying.
I inferred from his words that he was angry.
hint: C1: 'Imply' = suggest; 'infer' = deduce from evidence.

text: Choose the correct word: 'The two events are ____.'
hint: C1: All are synonyms.

text: Which sentence uses 'disinterested' and 'uninterested' correctly?
options: He was uninterested in the boring lecture.
She remained disinterested in the outcome.
hint: C1: 'Disinterested' = impartial; 'uninterested' = bored.

text: Choose the correct word: 'The plan was ____ to fail.'
hint: C1: All mean 'certain to' with different connotations.

text: Which sentence uses 'flaunt' and 'flout' correctly?
options: She flouted the rules without concern.
hint: C2: 'Flaunt' = show off; 'flout' = disregard rules.

text: Choose the correct word: 'The ____ of the mountain was breathtaking.'
hint: C1: 'Peak' = summit; 'peek' = quick look; 'pique' = irritation or interest.

text: Which sentence uses 'allusion' and 'illusion' correctly?
options: He made an allusion to Shakespeare.
The painting gives an illusion of depth.
hint: C1: 'Allusion' = reference; 'illusion' = false perception.

text: Choose the correct word: 'The theory is based on a ____.'
hint: C1: 'Fallacy' = mistaken belief; 'fallacious' = adjective.

text: Which sentence uses 'compliment' and 'complement' correctly?
options: She complimented him on his work.
The wine complements the cheese perfectly.
hint: C1: 'Compliment' = praise; 'complement' = complete or go well with.

text: Choose the correct word: 'He was ____ of the crime.'
hint: C1: All mean 'not responsible'.

text: Which sentence uses 'council' and 'counsel' correctly?
options: The city council approved the budget.
She counseled him to be careful.
hint: C1: 'Council' = governing body; 'counsel' = advice or lawyer.

text: Choose the correct word: 'The ____ of the story is unclear.'
hint: C1: All are possible.

text: Which sentence uses 'ensure', 'insure', and 'assure' correctly?
options: Please ensure that the door is locked.
I assure you that everything is fine.
The car is insured against theft.
hint: C1: 'Ensure' = make certain; 'assure' = convince; 'insure' = insurance.

text: Choose the correct word: 'He is a ____ of the truth.'
hint: C1: All are possible collocations.

text: Which sentence uses 'stationary' and 'stationery' correctly?
options: She bought new stationery for school.
The train was stationary for an hour.
hint: C1: 'Stationary' = not moving; 'stationery' = writing materials.

text: Choose the correct word: 'The ____ of the meeting was productive.'
hint: C1: All are synonyms.

text: Which sentence uses 'emigrate' and 'immigrate' correctly?
options: He emigrated from Italy to Canada.
She immigrated to the United States.
hint: C1: 'Emigrate from' = leave; 'immigrate to' = enter.

text: Choose the correct word: 'The evidence was ____.'
hint: C1: All mean 'not decisive'.

text: Which sentence uses 'who' and 'whom' correctly?
options: To whom should I address the letter?
hint: C1: 'Who' for subject; 'whom' for object (especially formal).

text: Choose the correct word: 'She is the person ____ I spoke.'
hint: C1: 'Whom' (formal) or 'that' (informal) after preposition omitted.

text: Which sentence uses 'that' and 'which' correctly in restrictive clauses?
options: The book that I read was excellent.
The book, which I read, was excellent.
The book which I read was excellent.
hint: C1: Restrictive clause = no commas, 'that' preferred; non-restrictive = commas, 'which'.

text: Choose the correct word: '____ of the candidates is qualified.'
hint: C1: 'None' can be singular or plural; 'not one' is singular.

text: Which sentence uses 'between' and 'among' correctly?
options: Divide the money between the two children.
Divide the money among the three children.
The secret was between you and me.
hint: C1: 'Between' for two; 'among' for three or more.

text: Choose the correct word: 'He was ____ of the team.'
hint: C1: All are possible.

text: Which sentence uses 'bring' and 'take' correctly?
options: Take the file to her office.
Please bring your passport when you come.
hint: C1: 'Bring' toward speaker; 'take' away from speaker.

text: Choose the correct word: 'The meeting was ____.'
hint: C1: All mean 'moved to later time'.

text: Which sentence uses 'can' and 'may' correctly for permission?
hint: C1: 'May' formal permission; 'can' informal/ability.

text: Choose the correct word: 'The project was ____.'
hint: C1: All are synonyms.

text: Which sentence uses 'lend' and 'borrow' correctly?
hint: C1: 'Lend' = give temporarily; 'borrow' = take temporarily.

text: Choose the correct word: 'She ____ a lot of time on social media.'
hint: C1: 'Spend time' and 'pass time' are common.

text: Which sentence uses 'rise' and 'raise' correctly?
options: The sun rises in the east.
hint: C1: 'Rise' = go up (no object); 'raise' = lift (needs object).

text: Choose the correct word: 'He ____ a decision.'
hint: C1: 'Make a decision', 'take a decision' (British), 'reach a decision'.

text: Which sentence uses 'lie' and 'lay' correctly?
options: She laid the book on the table.
He lay on the bed yesterday.
hint: C2: 'Lie' (recline): lie, lay, lain; 'lay' (place): lay, laid, laid.

text: Choose the correct word: 'The report was ____ in detail.'
hint: C1: All are possible.

text: Which sentence uses 'hang' correctly in past tense?
options: He hung the picture on the wall.
The coat was hanging on the hook.
hint: C2: 'Hung' for objects; 'hanged' for execution.

text: Choose the correct word: 'She ____ a promise.'
hint: C1: 'Make a promise', 'give a promise', 'keep a promise'.

text: Which sentence uses 'sink' and 'drown' correctly?
options: The ship sank in the ocean.
hint: C1: 'Sink' for objects; 'drown' for living things (die from water).

text: Choose the correct word: 'The company ____ a new CEO.'
hint: C1: All are possible.

text: Which sentence uses 'hear' and 'listen' correctly?
hint: C1: 'Hear' = perceive sound; 'listen' = pay attention.

text: Choose the correct word: 'She ____ the opportunity.'
hint: C1: All collocate with 'opportunity'.

text: Which sentence uses 'watch', 'see', and 'look' correctly?
options: I saw a bird in the tree.
I watched the movie last night.
hint: C1: 'See' = perceive; 'look' = direct gaze; 'watch' = observe over time.

text: Choose the correct word: 'The evidence ____ his guilt.'
hint: C1: All are possible.

text: Which sentence uses 'say' and 'tell' correctly?
options: He said that he was tired.
He told me that he was tired.
hint: C1: 'Say' = report words; 'tell' = inform someone.

text: Choose the correct word: 'The project was a ____.'
hint: C1: All possible depending on context.

text: Which sentence uses 'rob' and 'steal' correctly?
options: He stole money from the bank.
They robbed him of his wallet.
hint: C1: 'Rob' = take from person/place; 'steal' = take object.

text: Choose the correct word: 'She ____ a good impression.'
hint: C1: 'Make an impression' or 'create an impression'.

text: Which sentence uses 'remember' and 'remind' correctly?
options: Please remind me to call her.
He reminded me of my father.
hint: C1: 'Remember' = recall; 'remind' = cause to remember.

text: Choose the correct word: 'The ____ of the argument was weak.'
hint: C1: All mean 'underlying support'.

text: Which sentence uses 'win' and 'beat' correctly?
hint: C1: 'Win' = prize/game; 'beat' = defeat opponent.

text: Choose the correct word: 'He ____ a lot of money.'
hint: C1: All possible depending on context.

text: Which sentence uses 'hope' and 'wish' correctly?
options: I hope to see you soon.
I wish you a happy birthday.
hint: C1: 'Hope' for possible future; 'wish' for unreal/desire.

text: Choose the correct word: 'The ____ of the situation is critical.'
hint: C1: All are possible.

text: Which sentence uses 'refuse' and 'deny' correctly?
hint: C1: 'Refuse' = say no; 'deny' = say something is false.

text: Choose the correct word: 'She ____ the challenge.'
hint: C1: All collocate with 'challenge'.

text: Which sentence uses 'wait' and 'expect' correctly?
options: I expect him to arrive at 5 PM.
She is waiting for a response.
hint: C1: 'Wait' = pass time; 'expect' = anticipate.

text: Choose the correct word: 'The ____ of the plan was flawed.'
hint: C1: All are possible.

text: Which sentence uses 'care' correctly?
options: She cares for her elderly mother.
I don't care what you think.
hint: C1: 'Care about' = concerned; 'care for' = look after or like.

text: Choose the correct word: 'He ____ a strong argument.'
hint: C1: All collocate with 'argument'.

text: Which sentence uses 'persuade' and 'convince' correctly?
options: I convinced him that I was right.
She persuaded me to apply for the job.
hint: C1: 'Persuade to do', 'convince that' – though often used interchangeably.

text: Choose the correct word: 'The ____ of the meeting was positive.'
hint: C1: All are possible.

text: Which sentence uses 'avoid' and 'prevent' correctly?
options: He avoided the accident by braking.
hint: C1: 'Avoid' = stay away from; 'prevent' = stop from happening.

text: Choose the correct word: 'She ____ a good relationship with her colleagues.'
hint: C1: All collocate with 'relationship'.

text: Which sentence uses 'desire' and 'want' correctly?
options: I want a cup of coffee.
He desires a more meaningful life.
She desires to travel the world.
hint: C1: 'Desire' is more formal/stronger than 'want'.

text: Choose the correct word: 'The ____ of the research was groundbreaking.'
hint: C1: All are possible.

text: Which sentence uses 'require' and 'request' correctly?
options: I request your presence at the meeting.
The rules require all students to attend.
hint: C1: 'Require' = need/obligation; 'request' = ask for politely.

text: Choose the correct word: 'He ____ the consequences.'
hint: C1: All collocate with 'consequences'.

text: Which sentence uses 'remind' and 'remember' correctly?
options: Please remind me to call my mother.
I remember my first day of school.
The smell reminds me of my grandmother.
hint: C1: 'Remind' = cause to remember; 'remember' = recall.

text: Choose the correct word: 'The ____ of the matter is simple.'
hint: C1: All are possible.
